envoy: noun. A diplomatic agent of the second rank, next in status after an ambassador.
envy: noun. Resentful desire of something possessed by another or others (but not limited to material possessions).
eon: noun. Eternity, the duration of the universe.
erne: noun. A sea eagle (Haliaeetus), especially the white-tailed eagle (Haliaeetus albicilla)
eve: noun. The day or night before, usually used for holidays, such as Christmas Eve.
evener: adjective. comparative form of even: more even
every: determiner. All of a countable group (considered individually), without exception.
eye: noun. An organ through which animals see (“perceive surroundings via light”).
eyer: noun. One who eyes someone or something.
eyne: noun. Alternative form of eyen: plural of eye.
eyre: noun. A journey taken by certain Medieval English itinerant judges (justices in eyre)
nee: interjection. no, used to express no as a quantity, i.e. not any, like German kein /Dutch geen /French rien.
nerve: noun. A bundle of neurons with their connective tissue sheaths, blood vessels and lymphatics.
never: adverb. At no time; on no occasion; in no circumstance.
nor: conjunction. And... not (introducing a negative statement, without necessarily following one).
one: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 1.
ore: noun. Rock or other material that contains valuable or utilitarian materials; primarily a rock containing metals or gems for which it is
oven: noun. A chamber used for baking or heating.
oyer: noun. A hearing in a civil case which is based on the content of a document, in which the plaintiff is required to produce the document.
ree: noun. Alternative form of rei (“Portuguese real”).
reeve: noun. Any of several local officials, with varying responsibilities.
rev: verb. To increase the speed of a motor, or to operate at a higher speed.
rye: noun. A grain used extensively in Europe for making bread, beer, and (now generally) for animal fodder.
vee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter V / v.
veer: verb. To let out (a sail-line), to allow (a sheet) to run out.
veery: noun. An American thrush (Catharus fuscescens) common in the Northern United States and Canada.
veneer: noun. A thin decorative covering of fine material (usually wood) applied to coarser wood or other material.
venery: noun. The hunting of wild animals.
very: adverb. To a great extent or degree.
voe: noun. A narrow sea inlet, particularly in the Shetland Islands, similar to a fjord.
yen: noun. The unit of Japanese currency (symbol: ¥) since 1871, divided into 100 sen.
yon: determiner. Distant, but within sight; (that thing) just over there.
evene: verb. To occur; to happen; to come to pass.
evoe: interjection. Alternative form of euoi (“Bacchic cry”).
eyn: noun. Alternative form of eyen: plural of eye.
noy: verb. To annoy; to harm or injure.
nye: noun. A brood or flock of pheasants.
ory: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Odia.
overeye: verb. To superintend; inspect; observe; witness.
reen: noun. A running waterway that links a ditch or stream to a river.
veney: noun. A bout; a thrust; a venew.
yee: pronoun. You (the people being addressed); alternative spelling of ye.
yere: pronoun. your (plural); of ye, belonging to ye
yern: adverb. eagerly, heartily, gladly, willingly, earnestly.
yor: adjective. Eye dialect spelling of your.
